title: The Effects of Directed Therapeutic Intent on Live and Damaged Cells
description: This dataset documents a double-blind case study examining the physiological effects of directed therapeutic intent on live and damaged cells. A self-described energy medicine practitioner participated in 60 treatment sessions over 10 days, with concurrent collection of physiological data. Sessions included: 40 sessions with live cells, 10 sessions with no cells (medium only), and 10 sessions with dead/x-rayed cells. The study employed randomized cell presentation under conditions of experimenter and participant blinding to assess potential biofield effects on cellular systems. Matching control samples were treated by a sham therapist at a different location. Data were acquired at the MD Anderson Cancer Research Center.
methods_description: Data were collected during 60 total sessions over 10 days with ten sessions daily, each approximately 25 minutes. Each session consisted of five segments: 2-minute baseline (no cells), 5-minute treatment (still), 5-minute treatment (movement allowed), 5-minute treatment (still), and 2-minute post-treatment baseline. Conditions included: live cells (40 sessions), no cells/medium only (10 sessions), and dead/x-rayed cells (10 sessions). Both the practitioner and experimenter were blinded to cell type. Matching control samples were treated by a sham therapist at a different location.
